summary: Removed several unlinked Ness assembly source files from asm/melee/ft/chara/ftNess and adjusted local labels in asm/dolphin/mtx/mtx.s from global-style lbl_ names to assembler-local .L_ names. The PR was a cleanup with no discussion or review comments in the provided slice; its main value was pruning stale/unlinked asm fragments and preventing label namespace pollution in linked assembly.
what_changed: Deleted five unlinked Ness asm files: ftNess_2.s, ftNess_4.s, ftNess_5.s, ftNess_6.s, and ftNess_7.s.

The deleted Ness asm fragments contained functions such as ftNs_AttackS4_OnReflect, ftNs_AttackS4_Enter, ftNs_AttackLw4Release_Enter, ftNs_SpecialS_ItemPKFireSpawn, ftNs_SpecialS_Enter, ftNs_SpecialAirS_Enter, ftNs_SpecialAirNHold_Coll, and ftNs_SpecialAirNEnd_Coll, plus associated sdata2 constants.
Updated branch targets and labels inside asm/dolphin/mtx/mtx.s for PSMTXInverse and PSMTXRotTrig from lbl_80342394/lbl_803424B4/etc. to local labels .L_80342394/.L_803424B4/etc.
The mtx.s edits were label-name-only changes: 12 added lines and 12 deleted lines in the diff excerpt, preserving instruction addresses and branch destinations.
smart_moves: Pruned unlinked assembly rather than leaving stale source files that could mislead future decompilation work or conflict with regenerated/linked sources.
Converted internal branch labels in mtx.s to .L_ local labels, making it clearer that these labels are not exported symbols and should not participate in the global symbol namespace.
Kept mtx.s control flow intact while only changing label spelling, reducing risk for a cleanup PR.
decomp_lessons: When asm files are no longer linked, remove them instead of preserving outdated split markers, labels, and hand-written constants that can become false evidence for future matching.
Use local .L_ labels for intra-function branch targets in assembly when the labels are implementation details, especially in library asm such as dolphin/mtx.

Deleting unlinked asm can be a legitimate decomp cleanup step after code has moved elsewhere or is no longer part of the build, but the provided slice does not show the build-system context explaining why these particular Ness files were unlinked.
If an asm cleanup only renames labels, preserve exact branch offsets and instruction comments so reviewers can see that code generation/behavior is unchanged.
naming_conventions: Internal assembly labels should prefer .L_<address> style rather than global-looking lbl_<address> names when they are only local branch targets.
The deleted Ness asm used ftNs_ function prefixes for Ness fighter routines, but this PR does not introduce or review those names.

Constants in the deleted files used lbl_804Dxxxx names with inline float comments such as /*0.0f*/, /*1.0f*/, and /*13.0f*/; because the files were removed, these should not be treated as live naming guidance from this PR.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: For branch-only label cleanup, rename both the branch operands and the target label definitions in the same patch to avoid dangling labels.
Local labels in mtx.s were updated in PSMTXInverse and PSMTXRotTrig without changing opcode comments, e.g. bne lbl_80342394 became bne .L_80342394 at the same address.

The deleted Ness asm included old split-marker comments such as SPLIT NO LATER THAN HERE and SPLIT NO EARLIER THAN HERE; removing unlinked files also removes obsolete split guidance that could confuse later matching efforts.
review_feedback: No issue comments, review comments, or review feedback were present in the provided PR slice.
The PR merged quickly on the same day it was opened; no explicit reviewer rationale is available in the provided data.
follow_up_queries: Check surrounding PRs or build scripts to determine when and why the Ness asm files became unlinked.
Search for remaining global-style lbl_ branch targets in linked asm files that could be converted to .L_ local labels.
Verify whether deleted Ness routines had already been matched in C or moved to other linked asm files before this cleanup.
